paper_title: Materials and Methods of Chironomus perennis Study

methods.description: This method describes the collection and preservation of adult specimens, pharate pupae, and their exuviae for morphological analysis.

steps.description: The adult specimens were collected with a sweep net near rivers, streams and lakes; other adult specimens, pharate pupae and their exuviae -with a drift net; some samples were collected with light traps.
steps.searchable_summary: collect specimens using sweep nets, drift nets, and light traps

steps.description: The material was preserved in 70% ethanol.
The specimens were cleared in 10% KOH after the separation of wings and rinsed, including wings, in acetic acid, then transferred in xylene:phenol 3:1, finally mounted in Canada Balsam on slides.
steps.searchable_summary: clear specimens in KOH, rinse in acetic acid, transfer to xylene:phenol, mount on slides

methods.description: This method involves the examination and description of morphological features of the collected specimens.

steps.description: Morphological terminology and abbreviations follow Saether (1980). All measures are in µm unless otherwise stated.
steps.searchable_summary: use Saether (1980) for morphological terminology and measurements

steps.description: Description based on material examined and collected in Italy. Medium sized to large species 3.0-3.9mm, with wing length 2.0-2.4 mm.
steps.searchable_summary: describe species size and wing length
